Package de.uka.ilkd.key.settings

Class FeatureSettings

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.settings.AbstractSettings
de.uka.ilkd.key.settings.FeatureSettings
All Implemented Interfaces:
Settings
public class FeatureSettings extends AbstractSettings
Feature flags for the selected and deselected of experimental features.

In the old days of KeY, we used to have --experimental flag, which was a bad idea. You cannot control which feature you want to activate. Instead, you are getting the full load of fun(ctionality). This class allows you to have more fine-grained feature flags by defining feature flags which are controllable by the user.

    • on

      public static void on(FeatureSettings.Feature feature, Consumer<Boolean> update)
      Helper function for notification on feature flag changes.
      Parameters:
      feature - the feature to be listening on
      update - a callback function which gets informed on changes with the new value

    • onAndActivate

      public static void onAndActivate(FeatureSettings.Feature feature, Consumer<Boolean> update)
      Helper function for notification on feature flag changes which also calls the consumer.
      Parameters:
      feature - the feature to be listening on
      update - a callback function which gets informed on changes with the new value

    • readSettings

      public void readSettings(Properties props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      gets a Properties object and has to perform the necessary steps in order to change this object in a way that it represents the stored settings

    • writeSettings

      public void writeSettings(Properties props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      The settings to store are written to the given Properties object.
      Parameters:
      props - the Properties object where to write the settings as (key, value) pair

    • readSettings

      public void readSettings(@NonNull Configuration props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      This method transfers the given configuration information into the local states. The setter methods are used so PropertyChangeEvent should be triggered accordingly to the new state.

      Parameters:
      props - a non-null references to a configuration object. The state of this object shall not be changed by the implementations.

    • writeSettings

      public void writeSettings(@NonNull Configuration props)
      Description copied from interface: Settings
      The internal state is stored in the given configuration object. The stored information must be sufficient to restore the local state.

      The internal state shall not be changed by the implementations.

      Parameters:
      props - a non-null reference to a configration object, which state is modified accordingly to the local internal state.
